rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL K. ROBERTSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Renew Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 31, 2007, LINE 4, DEFENDANT NIBCO INC.'S Motion To Renew Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication IS DENIED. THE PURPOSE OF G.O. 129 IS TO FACILITATE DISCOVERY AND THE PARTIES' ABILITY TO RESPOND TO MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. TO GRANT THE RELIEF REQUESTED WOULD UNDERCUT THE OBJECTIVE OF G.O. 129 AND DEFENDANT HAS NEVER ADDRESSED ITS FAILURE TO FILE TIMELY G.O. 129 RESPONSES. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK RIORDAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 31, 2007, LINE 16, DEFENDANT MISSION STUCCO COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF IDENTIFIES DEFENDANT IN HIS DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND ADDS DETAILS IN HIS DECLARATION. THE OBJECTION TO THE HERNANDEZ DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED FOR LACK OF FOUNDATION. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JANET PICKENS VS. BRENDA HUTCHINSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion To Set Aside Default And Quash Service Of Summons

RELIEF IS MANDATORY UNDER CCP SECTION 473 BASED ON THE AFFIDAVIT OF DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL. IT ALSO APPEARS THAT SERVICE WAS IMPROPER. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: RICARDO PAEZ VS. SEIU LOCAL 790 et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 31, 2007, LINE 6, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1021's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. THE MOTION IS CONTINUED FOR FURTHER BRIEFING AND HEARING. THE PARTIES ARE TO SUBMIT SIMULTANEOUS BRIEFS ON THE SUBJECTS DESCRIBED BELOW ON NOVEMBER 9, 2007 AND REPLY BRIEFS ON NOVEMBER 16, 207. THE HEARING IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 20, 2007. 1) IS THE PERB DECISION OF AUGUST 10, 2005 A FINAL DECISION OF THAT BODY? IF SO, WHAT BASIS DOES PLAINTIFF HAVE TO BRING THIS CLAIM? 2) THE PERB HAS EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION TO MAKE THE INITIAL DETERMINATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. ISN'T PLAINTIFF'S ONLY REMEDY AFTER THAT DECISION, IS A WRIT FOR ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE? HAS THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS RUN ON SUCH A WRIT? =(302/PJM/DD-X)

rulings.Case Title: JACKIE CRIVINAR VS. PARSONS CORPORATION A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Parsons Corp.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of The Proceedings (Transferred From Dept. 301 Per Judge Busch'S Recusal)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 31, 2007, LINE 15, Defendant Parsons Corp.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of The Proceedings IS DENIED. GIVEN THE MANNER IN WHICH THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO, IT IS A CONTRACT OF ADHESION AND PROCEDURALLY UNCONSCIONABLE WITHIN THE MEANING OF ARMENDARIZ. IT ALSO IS SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE WITHIN THE MEANING OF MERCURO V. SUPERIOR COURT (2002) 96 CAL. APP. 4TH 167; INGLE V. CIRCUIT CITY INC. (9TH CIR. 2003) 328 F. 3D 1165. THE AGREEMENT LACKS MUTUALITY IN REQUIRING EMPLOYEE CLAIMS TO BE SUBMITTED TO ARBITRATION WHILE PERSERVING THE RIGHT OF THE EMPLOYER TO SEEK RELIEF IN COURT ON THE EMPLOYER'S LIKELY CLAIMS. THE AGREEMENT ALSO AUTHORIZES THE EMPLOYER TO UNILATERALLY AMEND OR TERMINATE THE AGREEMENT. THE LIMITATIONS ON DISCOVERY ARE PROBLEMATIC. MARTINEZ V. MASTER PROTECTION CORP.; (2004) 118 CAL. APP. 4TH 107, 119. TAKEN TOGETHER, UNCONSCIONABILITY PERMEATES THE AGREEMENT AND SEVERANCE OF PROVISIONS IS NOT APPROPRIATE. =(302/PJM/AL)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE PIERI VS. CHRISTOPHER DOLAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint After Reclassification As An Ordidnary Civil Action;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 31, 2007, LINE 11, DEFENDANTS LENT HOWARD AND CHRISTOPHER DOLAN'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint After Reclassification As An Ordidnary Civil Action IS GRANTED. BECAUSE THIS IS A COMPULSORY COUNTERCLAIM UNDER CCP SECTION 426.30, LEAVE MAY BE DENIED ONLY UPON A SHOWING OF BAD FAITH, NOT SIMPLY BAD JUDGMENT BUT "THE CONSCIOUS DOING OF A WRONG BECAUSE OF A DISHONEST PURPOSE OR MORAL OBLIQUITY." SILVER ORGANIZATIONS LTD. V. FRANK (1990) 217CAL.APP.3D 94. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS DO NOT MEET THIS STANDARD. =(302/PJM/AY)
